title: Implement halt-loop progress checkpoint
description: Canonical shape of .claude/knowledge-base/implementations/.progress-{slug}.json — the runtime checkpoint the /implement halt-loop writes each iteration and every gate reads. This file is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the schema: implementation-prompt.md's PROGRESS step and check_progress_schema.py both follow it. The gates read `data['tasks']` (an array) and filter each task by `phase`, so a bare task object or a task without `phase`/`id` is NOT consumable.

slug.description: Plan slug this checkpoint belongs to.

tasks.description: One entry per plan task, in dependency order. Gates iterate this array — it MUST be present even with a single task.

previous_halt.description: Set on resume after a recovered blocker (resume-protocol.md step 3).

id.description: Plan task id, e.g. 'T1.1'. Read by check_phase_completeness as t['id'] — NOT 'task_id'.

phase.description: Phase number, e.g. '1'. Gates filter by str(task['phase']); omitting it makes the task invisible to every phase-scoped gate.

status.description: Current TDD-cycle stage. A task is complete only at 'committed' (or honestly 'blocked').

files.description: Files this task created/edited. Read by check_diff_cohesion to detect scope drift. Should match the plan task's '#### Files to edit'.

commit_sha.description: Commit SHA once status='committed'. Read by check_diff_cohesion and diff_symbols to derive the real diff. Null/absent until committed.

wiring.description: Self-reported wiring triad result. NOTE: run_validation re-verifies pillar (a) independently — this field is an audited claim, not trusted evidence.

blocked_reason.description: Required when status='blocked' — explicit reason + recommended human action.
